chunks.title: Creation of Light and Day One
chunks.reason: Describes the creation of light and the first day.

chunks.title: Creation of the Expanse and Day Two
chunks.reason: Describes the creation of the sky and the second day.

chunks.title: Creation of Land and Vegetation Day Three
chunks.reason: Describes the gathering of waters, land, and plants.

chunks.title: Creation of Celestial Bodies Day Four
chunks.reason: Describes the creation of sun, moon, and stars.

chunks.title: Creation of Sea Creatures and Birds Day Five
chunks.reason: Describes the creation of aquatic life and birds.

chunks.title: Creation of Land Animals and Man Day Six
chunks.reason: Describes the creation of land animals, humans, and the conclusion.

chunks.title: Completion of Creation
chunks.reason: Describes the conclusion of the creation week and the sanctification of the seventh day.

chunks.title: Creation of Man and Garden
chunks.reason: Narrates the formation of man, the planting of Eden, and the first divine command.

chunks.title: Creation of Woman
chunks.reason: Details God's provision of a helper, the naming of animals, and the formation of Eve.

chunks.title: The Temptation and Fall
chunks.reason: A continuous dialogue between the serpent and the woman followed by their actions of eating the fruit.

chunks.title: Hiding and Confrontation
chunks.reason: The narrative of the couple hiding from God and the subsequent questioning by Yahweh.

chunks.title: Judgments Pronounced
chunks.reason: A series of divine judgments and curses pronounced against the serpent, the woman, and the man.

chunks.title: Clothing and Expulsion
chunks.reason: The naming of Eve, God providing garments, and the final expulsion from the garden.

chunks.title: Cain and Abel Narrative
chunks.reason: Complete narrative of the first murder, judgment, and exile.

chunks.title: Lineage of Cain
chunks.reason: Genealogical record of Cain's descendants including Lamech's speech.

chunks.title: Birth of Seth and Enosh
chunks.reason: Narrative conclusion introducing the line of Seth and worship.

chunks.title: Adam's Genealogy
chunks.reason: Complete account of Adam's life and descendants.

chunks.title: Seth to Jared Lineage
chunks.reason: Continuous list of generations from Seth to Jared.

chunks.title: Jared to Enoch Lineage
chunks.reason: Continuation of the line including Enoch's unique departure.

chunks.title: Methuselah to Lamech Lineage
chunks.reason: Continuation of the line through Methuselah and Lamech.

chunks.title: Noah's Birth
chunks.reason: Introduction of Noah as the next generation head.

chunks.title: The Sons of God and Noah's Favor
chunks.reason: Describes the intermarriage of divine beings and men, God's judgment on human lifespan, and introduces Noah.

chunks.title: Noah's Righteousness and the Decree of Judgment
chunks.reason: Establishes Noah's character contrasted with the earth's corruption and God's decision to destroy all flesh.

chunks.title: Instructions for the Ark
chunks.reason: Contains specific architectural details and commands given by God to Noah regarding the construction of the ark.

chunks.title: Noah's Obedience
chunks.reason: Concludes the section by stating Noah's faithful execution of God's commands.

chunks.title: Command to Enter the Ark
chunks.reason: Yahweh's direct instruction to Noah regarding the ark and animals, followed by Noah's immediate obedience.

chunks.title: Noah Enters the Ark
chunks.reason: Narrative account of Noah and his family entering the ark after the seven-day wait.

chunks.title: The Flood Begins
chunks.reason: Detailed description of the floodgates opening, the rain falling, and the animals entering with Noah.

chunks.title: The Waters Prevail
chunks.reason: Description of the waters rising, covering the mountains, and destroying all life except those in the ark.

chunks.title: The Waters Recede
chunks.reason: Describes the subsiding of the flood waters and the ark resting on Ararat.

chunks.title: Noah Sends Out Birds
chunks.reason: Details Noah's testing of the earth with a raven and dove until the ground dried.

chunks.title: God Commands Exit
chunks.reason: Contains God's direct instruction for Noah and the animals to leave the ark.

chunks.title: Noah's Sacrifice and God's Promise
chunks.reason: Combines Noah's offering with God's oracle regarding the seasons and covenant.

chunks.title: Blessing and Food Laws
chunks.reason: God's speech establishing the new creation order with dietary laws and the institution of capital punishment.

chunks.title: Covenant with Noah
chunks.reason: God establishes an everlasting covenant with Noah and all living creatures, signified by the rainbow.

chunks.title: Sons of Noah
chunks.reason: A brief genealogical list identifying the three sons and the lineage of Ham.

chunks.title: Noah's Drunkenness and Curse
chunks.reason: A narrative account of Noah planting a vineyard, becoming drunk, and his subsequent blessing and curse upon his sons.

chunks.title: Noah's Death
chunks.reason: A concluding summary of Noah's lifespan after the flood and his death.

chunks.title: Introduction and Japheth's Line
chunks.reason: Introduces the table of nations and lists the descendants of Japheth.

chunks.title: Descendants of Ham
chunks.reason: Details the families of Cush, Mizraim, and Canaan with specific geographical notes.

chunks.title: Descendants of Shem
chunks.reason: Lists the lineage of Shem through Arpachshad and Joktan.

chunks.title: Summary of Noah's Sons
chunks.reason: Concludes the chapter by summarizing the separation of nations from Noah's family.

chunks.title: The Tower of Babel
chunks.reason: A complete narrative unit describing the construction of the tower and God's judgment.

chunks.title: Genealogy from Shem to Terah
chunks.reason: A continuous list of descendants tracing the line from Shem to Terah.

chunks.title: The Generations of Terah
chunks.reason: A narrative section detailing the family of Terah and their move to Haran.

chunks.title: Call and Journey to Canaan
chunks.reason: Covers the divine call, departure from Haran, arrival in Canaan, and altar building.

chunks.title: Famine and Sojourn in Egypt
chunks.reason: Covers the famine, the deception regarding Sarai, Pharaoh's plagues, and Abram's departure.

chunks.title: Abram and Lot Return to Canaan
chunks.reason: Narrative of their journey back from Egypt and the resulting conflict over resources.

chunks.title: Separation of Abram and Lot
chunks.reason: Dialogue between the two men followed by Lot's choice and their separation.

chunks.title: God's Promise to Abram
chunks.reason: Divine speech promising the land and numerous descendants to Abram.

chunks.title: Abram Builds an Altar
chunks.reason: Narrative conclusion describing Abram's new location and worship.

chunks.title: War of the Kings and Capture of Lot
chunks.reason: Describes the political conflict between eastern and southern kings, the battle in the Salt Sea, and the abduction of Lot.

chunks.title: Abram's Rescue Mission
chunks.reason: Narrates Abram hearing the news, gathering his men, pursuing the enemy to Dan and Hobah, and recovering Lot.

chunks.title: Blessing by Melchizedek
chunks.reason: Records the encounter with Melchizedek, his offering of bread and wine, and the priestly blessing given to Abram.

chunks.title: Abram's Exchange with the King of Sodom
chunks.reason: Contains the negotiation between the king of Sodom and Abram regarding the division of spoils.

chunks.title: Promise of Heir and Faith
chunks.reason: Abram questions God about his heir and receives the promise that his own offspring will inherit, followed by his belief being counted as righteousness.

chunks.title: Covenant of the Land and Future Sojourn
chunks.reason: God identifies Himself as the deliverer from Ur, promises the land, answers Abram's question about possession through a prophetic vision of future slavery and return.

chunks.title: Ritual Covenant and Land Boundaries
chunks.reason: The formal covenant ceremony occurs with the smoking oven and torch passing between pieces, concluding with a specific list of nations whose land is given to Abram's seed.

chunks.title: Sarai and Hagar's Conflict
chunks.reason: Narrative of Sarai giving Hagar to Abram, the resulting tension, and Hagar fleeing.

chunks.title: The Angel's Encounter with Hagar
chunks.reason: Dialogue between the angel and Hagar including the oracle of her son's name and future.

chunks.title: Birth of Ishmael
chunks.reason: Narrative conclusion detailing the birth of Hagar's son and Abram's age.

chunks.title: God's Covenant Promise to Abraham
chunks.reason: Divine declaration establishing the covenant, name change, and land promise.

chunks.title: The Law of Circumcision
chunks.reason: Specific command regarding the sign of the covenant and its requirements.

chunks.title: Promise of Isaac and Blessing of Ishmael
chunks.reason: Exchange between God and Abraham regarding Sarah's son and Ishmael's future.

chunks.title: Abraham's Obedience in Circumcision
chunks.reason: Narrative account of Abraham and his household performing the command.

chunks.title: Abraham and the Three Visitors
chunks.reason: Narrative of Abraham's hospitality and the promise of Isaac.

chunks.title: Yahweh's Decision Regarding Sodom
chunks.reason: Transition from the visitors leaving to Yahweh's internal deliberation.

chunks.title: Abraham's Plea for Sodom
chunks.reason: Extended negotiation between Abraham and Yahweh regarding the city.

chunks.title: Lot and the Angels in Sodom
chunks.reason: Describes the arrival of angels, Lot's hospitality, and the city's siege.

chunks.title: Escape from Sodom
chunks.reason: Covers Lot's hurried departure, the destruction of the cities, and his wife turning to salt.

chunks.title: Lot in Zoar and the Cave
chunks.reason: Details Lot's move to Zoar, his retreat to a cave, and the birth of Moab and Ben-ammi.

chunks.title: Abraham in Gerar and God's Warning
chunks.reason: Narrative unit covering Abraham's arrival, the deception about Sarah, and God's intervention to Abimelech.

chunks.title: Abimelech Confronts Abraham and Restores Sarah
chunks.reason: Sequence of dialogue between Abimelech and Abraham followed by the restitution of Sarah and gifts.

chunks.title: Abraham's Prayer and Healing
chunks.reason: Concluding narrative describing the prayer that restores fertility to Abimelech's household.

chunks.title: Birth and Weaning of Isaac
chunks.reason: Narrative covering the fulfillment of God's promise regarding Isaac's birth and weaning.

chunks.title: Hagar and Ishmael in the Wilderness
chunks.reason: Story of Sarah's demand, Hagar's expulsion, God's provision, and Ishmael's growth.

chunks.title: Covenant at Beersheba
chunks.reason: Dialogue and narrative of the covenant between Abraham and Abimelech regarding the well.

chunks.title: The Sacrifice of Isaac
chunks.reason: A complete narrative unit covering the testing of Abraham and the provision of the ram.

chunks.title: The Divine Oath
chunks.reason: A distinct speech by the angel containing God's oath and promise of blessing.

chunks.title: Return to Beersheba and Genealogy
chunks.reason: A concluding narrative segment that transitions into a genealogical list of Nahor's children.

chunks.title: Death of Sarah
chunks.reason: Narrative introduction of Sarah's age and death in Hebron.

chunks.title: Purchase of the Cave
chunks.reason: Extended negotiation between Abraham and the sons of Heth regarding the burial site.

chunks.title: Completion of Purchase
chunks.reason: Final legal confirmation and transfer of title for the field and cave.

chunks.title: Abraham's Later Years and Keturah
chunks.reason: Describes Abraham taking Keturah as a wife, her sons, and the distribution of his estate between Isaac and the other sons.

chunks.title: Death and Burial of Abraham
chunks.reason: Records the lifespan of Abraham, his death, and the burial by Isaac and Ishmael in the cave of Machpelah.

chunks.title: Generations of Ishmael
chunks.reason: Lists the twelve sons of Ishmael and describes their settlement and lifespan after Abraham's death.

chunks.title: Birth of Jacob and Esau
chunks.reason: Details Isaac's marriage to Rebekah, her pregnancy, the oracle regarding the twins, and their birth.

chunks.title: Jacob and Esau as Adults
chunks.reason: Contrasts the characters of the brothers and narrates the sale of Esau's birthright for a bowl of stew.

chunks.title: Isaac goes to Gerar
chunks.reason: Narrative introduction of Isaac moving to Gerar and receiving God's initial instruction.

chunks.title: Isaac's deception revealed
chunks.reason: The story of Isaac claiming Rebekah as his sister and Abimelech discovering the truth.

chunks.title: Isaac's prosperity and departure
chunks.reason: Description of Isaac's wealth, the Philistines' jealousy, and his move to the valley.

chunks.title: Dug wells and God's appearance
chunks.reason: The narrative of digging wells named Esek, Sitnah, and Rehoboth, followed by a vision at Beersheba.

chunks.title: Covenant with Abimelech
chunks.reason: Dialogue and agreement between Isaac and Abimelech establishing a peace treaty.

chunks.title: Naming of Beersheba and Esau's wives
chunks.reason: The discovery of the final well leading to the city name and the introduction of Esau's marriages.

chunks.title: Isaac's Plan to Bless Esau
chunks.reason: Establishes the setting and Isaac's request for game before his death.

chunks.title: Rebekah's Scheme with Jacob
chunks.reason: Contains the conversation between Rebekah and Jacob and their preparation for the deception.

chunks.title: Jacob Deceives Isaac and Receives Blessing
chunks.reason: Narrates the encounter where Jacob impersonates Esau and receives the patriarchal blessing.

chunks.title: Esau's Return and Lament
chunks.reason: Describes Esau's arrival, Isaac's reaction, the second blessing for Esau, and Esau's anger.

chunks.title: Rebekah Sends Jacob to Haran
chunks.reason: Concludes with Rebekah's instruction for Jacob to flee and her complaint about the local women.

chunks.title: Isaac's Blessing and Esau's Reaction
chunks.reason: Combines Isaac's command to Jacob with the narrative of Esau's response.

chunks.title: Jacob's Dream at Bethel
chunks.reason: Covers the journey to Haran, the dream of the ladder, and Jacob's reaction.

chunks.title: Jacob's Vow
chunks.reason: Contains the specific vow and promise made by Jacob to God.

chunks.title: Jacob Arrives at Haran
chunks.reason: Narrative of Jacob's arrival, meeting the shepherds, and reunion with Laban.

chunks.title: Jacob's Service for Rachel
chunks.reason: Dialogue between Jacob and Laban regarding wages and the wedding deception.

chunks.title: Birth of Leah's Sons
chunks.reason: Narrative of God opening Leah's womb and the birth of her four sons.

chunks.title: Rachel and Bilhah's Children
chunks.reason: Describes Rachel giving her maid Bilhah to Jacob and the birth of Dan and Naphtali.

chunks.title: Leah and Zilpah's Children
chunks.reason: Describes Leah giving her maid Zilpah to Jacob and the birth of Gad and Asher.

chunks.title: Mandrakes and Leah's Children
chunks.reason: Narrates the exchange of mandrakes between Rachel and Leah leading to the birth of Issachar, Zebulun, and Dinah.

chunks.title: Rachel's Son Joseph
chunks.reason: Describes God opening Rachel's womb and the birth of Joseph.

chunks.title: Jacob Requests Departure
chunks.reason: Contains the negotiation between Jacob and Laban regarding wages for leaving.

chunks.title: Jacob's Flocks Increase
chunks.reason: Details the separation of flocks and Jacob's method to increase his wealth.

chunks.title: Jacob's Decision to Return
chunks.reason: Narrative introduction of the conflict and divine command.

chunks.title: Conversation with Rachel and Leah
chunks.reason: Jacob explains his situation to his wives and their response.

chunks.title: The Flight and Pursuit
chunks.reason: Jacob's departure, Rachel's theft of idols, and Laban's pursuit.

chunks.title: Laban Confronts Jacob
chunks.reason: Divine warning to Laban and the search for the stolen idols.

chunks.title: Jacob's Defense
chunks.reason: Jacob details his twenty years of service and grievances against Laban.

chunks.title: The Covenant at Mizpah
chunks.reason: Laban's response, the establishment of the boundary marker, and their parting.

chunks.title: Jacob meets the angels at Mahanaim
chunks.reason: Describes Jacob's arrival and naming of the place after seeing God's camp.

chunks.title: Jacob sends messengers to Esau
chunks.reason: Narrates the sending of envoys and Jacob's reaction to their report.

chunks.title: Jacob's prayer for deliverance
chunks.reason: Contains Jacob's direct address to God recalling promises and asking for safety.

chunks.title: Jacob prepares the present for Esau
chunks.reason: Details the inventory of gifts and instructions given to servants.

chunks.title: Jacob crosses the Jabbok
chunks.reason: Describes the movement of his family and possessions across the stream.

chunks.title: Jacob wrestles with God
chunks.reason: Narrates the wrestling match, name change to Israel, and naming of Peniel.

chunks.title: Jacob's limp and the dietary law
chunks.reason: Concludes the narrative with Jacob's physical state and the resulting custom.

chunks.title: Jacob meets Esau
chunks.reason: A continuous narrative of the reunion between Jacob and Esau including their dialogue and actions.

chunks.title: Jacob settles in Canaan
chunks.reason: A summary of Jacob's journey to Succoth and Shechem, including his purchase of land and establishment of an altar.

chunks.title: Dinah Violated and Jacob Silent
chunks.reason: Establishes the incident of Dinah's violation and Jacob's initial silence while his sons are away.

chunks.title: Hamor's Proposal and Sons' Deceptive Condition
chunks.reason: Contains the negotiation between Hamor and Jacob's sons regarding marriage and circumcision.

chunks.title: The City Agrees to Circumcision
chunks.reason: Describes the persuasion of the city men and their collective decision to undergo circumcision.

chunks.title: The Slaughter and Plunder
chunks.reason: Narrates the surprise attack by Simeon and Levi on the third day and the subsequent looting.

chunks.title: Jacob's Rebuke and Sons' Defense
chunks.reason: Concludes the chapter with Jacob's complaint about the trouble caused and the brothers' justification.

chunks.title: Jacob goes to Bethel
chunks.reason: Narrative of Jacob's journey to Bethel, purification of the household, and building an altar.

chunks.title: God renames Jacob
chunks.reason: Divine appearance where God blesses Jacob and changes his name to Israel.

chunks.title: Death of Rachel
chunks.reason: Narrative of Rachel's difficult birth, death, and burial near Bethlehem.

chunks.title: Reuben's sin and sons listed
chunks.reason: Brief account of Reuben's sin followed by a complete list of Jacob's twelve sons.

chunks.title: Death of Isaac
chunks.reason: Narrative of Jacob returning to Isaac and the death and burial of Isaac.

chunks.title: Esau's Departure to Seir
chunks.reason: Narrative account of Esau moving from Canaan to Edom due to overcrowding.

chunks.title: Sons and Chiefs of Esau
chunks.reason: Detailed list of Esau's sons by his wives and their descendants.

chunks.title: The Horites in Seir
chunks.reason: Catalog of the pre-existing inhabitants and their chiefs in Edom.

chunks.title: Kings of Edom Before Israel
chunks.reason: Sequential record of the eight kings who reigned in Edom.

chunks.title: Chiefs of Edom
chunks.reason: Final list of tribal chiefs and their locations in the land.

chunks.title: Joseph's Dreams and Family Tension
chunks.reason: Establishes Joseph's favored status and the brothers' jealousy through two dreams.

chunks.title: Joseph Sent to Shechem
chunks.reason: Jacob sends Joseph to check on his brothers and the flock.

chunks.title: The Brothers Plot Against Joseph
chunks.reason: Joseph arrives at Dothan and is stripped and cast into a pit.

chunks.title: Sale to the Ishmaelites
chunks.reason: A caravan arrives and Judah suggests selling Joseph instead of killing him.

chunks.title: Reuben's Return and Jacob's Grief
chunks.reason: The brothers deceive Jacob with the tunic and Joseph mourns his son.

chunks.title: Joseph in Egypt
chunks.reason: Concludes the narrative with Joseph's arrival in Egypt as a slave.

chunks.title: Judah's Sons and Tamar's Widowhood
chunks.reason: Narrative covering Judah's marriage of his sons to Tamar and the deaths of Er and Onan.

chunks.title: Judah and Tamar at Enaim
chunks.reason: Narrative of Judah's widowhood, Tamar's disguise, the encounter, and the resolution.

chunks.title: Birth of Perez and Zerah
chunks.reason: Narrative describing the birth of Tamar's twins.

chunks.title: Joseph in Potiphar's House
chunks.reason: Describes Joseph's rise to authority and Yahweh's blessing on Potiphar's household.

chunks.title: Joseph and Potiphar's Wife
chunks.reason: Narrates the temptation of Joseph by his master's wife and his flight.

chunks.title: Joseph Accused and Imprisoned
chunks.reason: Details the wife's false accusation using Joseph's garment and his subsequent imprisonment.

chunks.title: Joseph in Prison
chunks.reason: Describes Yahweh's presence with Joseph and his appointment as overseer of the jail.

chunks.title: Joseph appointed over the prisoners
chunks.reason: Describes the imprisonment of the officials and Joseph's appointment as their overseer.

chunks.title: Joseph interprets the dreams
chunks.reason: Contains the recounting of both dreams and Joseph's interpretations for each official.

chunks.title: Fulfillment of the dreams
chunks.reason: Narrates the events on Pharaoh's birthday and the final outcome for both officials.

chunks.title: Pharaoh's Dreams
chunks.reason: Describes the two dreams of Pharaoh and his initial reaction.

chunks.title: Joseph Summoned
chunks.reason: The cupbearer recalls Joseph's gift and Pharaoh sends for him.

chunks.title: Pharaoh Relates the Dream
chunks.reason: Pharaoh recounts the dream details to Joseph.

chunks.title: Joseph's Interpretation and Plan
chunks.reason: Joseph interprets the dreams and proposes a management plan.

chunks.title: Joseph's Promotion
chunks.reason: Pharaoh accepts the plan and elevates Joseph to power.

chunks.title: Years of Plenty and Family
chunks.reason: Joseph administers the land during plenty and has children.

chunks.title: The Famine Begins
chunks.reason: The famine arrives and Joseph's grain stores sustain Egypt.

chunks.title: Jacob Sends Sons to Egypt
chunks.reason: Describes the family's decision to travel to Egypt for grain due to famine.

chunks.title: Joseph Tests His Brothers
chunks.reason: Records the initial confrontation and Joseph's accusation of spying.

chunks.title: Joseph Imprisons and Releases Brothers
chunks.reason: Details the three-day confinement, the selection of Simeon, and the release with grain.

chunks.title: The Brothers Discover the Money
chunks.reason: Narrates the journey home and the discovery of their silver in the sacks.

chunks.title: Jacob's Reaction to the News
chunks.reason: Captures Jacob's despair and Reuben's offer regarding Benjamin.

chunks.title: Judah's Plea and Israel's Instructions
chunks.reason: A continuous exchange of dialogue between the brothers and their father regarding the journey to Egypt.

chunks.title: Arrival in Egypt and Preparation
chunks.reason: Narrative sequence describing the brothers' arrival, Joseph's command to the steward, and the men's preparation.

chunks.title: Joseph's Reunion with His Brothers
chunks.reason: The narrative climax where Joseph meets his brothers, reveals emotion over Benjamin, and serves the meal.

chunks.title: The Silver Cup Found
chunks.reason: Narrative of Joseph's plan to test his brothers and the discovery of the cup in Benjamin's sack.

chunks.title: Confrontation at Joseph's House
chunks.reason: Dialogue between the brothers and Joseph regarding their guilt and the fate of the cup bearer.

chunks.title: Judah's Plea for Benjamin
chunks.reason: Extended speech by Judah pleading with Joseph to let Benjamin return to their father.

chunks.title: Joseph Reveals Himself to His Brothers
chunks.reason: Narrative unit covering Joseph's emotional revelation and reconciliation with his brothers.

chunks.title: Pharaoh's Decree and Joseph's Gifts
chunks.reason: Dialogue between Pharaoh and Joseph followed by Joseph's instructions and gifts to his brothers.

chunks.title: Jacob's Reunion with Joseph
chunks.reason: Narrative unit describing the journey to Canaan and Jacob's emotional revival upon hearing the news.

chunks.title: Jacob's Journey to Egypt
chunks.reason: Describes the departure from Beersheba and arrival in Egypt with family and possessions.

chunks.title: Genealogy of Jacob's Family
chunks.reason: Lists the names of all sons and descendants who came to Egypt.

chunks.title: Joseph Meets Jacob
chunks.reason: Narrates Judah's guidance to Goshen and the emotional reunion between Joseph and Israel.

chunks.title: Joseph's Instructions for Pharaoh
chunks.reason: Contains Joseph's dialogue instructing his family on how to present themselves to the king.

chunks.title: Joseph Introduces His Family to Pharaoh
chunks.reason: Narrative of Joseph presenting his brothers and father to the king, receiving permission to settle in Goshen.

chunks.title: The Famine and Joseph's Economic Reform
chunks.reason: Account of the famine consuming resources, Egyptians selling livestock and land to Joseph for food.

chunks.title: Israel Settles in Goshen and Jacob's Deathbed Request
chunks.reason: Description of Israel's prosperity in Egypt followed by Jacob's final instructions to Joseph regarding his burial.

chunks.title: Jacob's Recall of Rachel and Joseph's Arrival
chunks.reason: Narrative introduction covering Joseph's arrival and Jacob's recollection of Rachel's death.

chunks.title: Joseph Brings Sons to Jacob for Blessing
chunks.reason: Dialogue and action describing the positioning of Ephraim and Manasseh before Jacob.

chunks.title: Jacob's Blessing of the Grandsons
chunks.reason: The formal blessing speech and the specific act of crossing hands to favor Ephraim.

chunks.title: Jacob's Final Words to Joseph
chunks.reason: Jacob's final instructions regarding his death and the gift of Shechem to Joseph.

chunks.title: Introduction to the Blessings
chunks.reason: Jacob summons his sons and sets the context for the prophecy.

chunks.title: Judgment on Reuben, Simeon, and Levi
chunks.reason: Specific oracles regarding the first three sons focusing on their character flaws.

chunks.title: Oracle on Judah
chunks.reason: A distinct prophetic section concerning the lineage and future of Judah.

chunks.title: Oracles on the Remaining Tribes
chunks.reason: Sequential prophecies for Zebulun through Benjamin forming a complete catalog.

chunks.title: Conclusion and Death of Jacob
chunks.reason: Narrative summary of the blessings followed by Jacob's final commands and death.

chunks.title: Joseph's Funeral Procession
chunks.reason: Describes the embalming, journey to Canaan, and burial of Jacob.

chunks.title: Brothers Seek Forgiveness
chunks.reason: The brothers fear Joseph's revenge and receive his reassurance.

chunks.title: Joseph's Death and Legacy
chunks.reason: Covers Joseph's final years, his prophecy about the Exodus, and his burial.
